Do not merge sub-tasks, skip sub-tasks, or add sub-tasks not listed in the decomposition.\n- **Spawn the synthesizer as a sub-agent.** If `decomposition.yaml` defines a sub-task with `parallel_group: reduce` or `parallel_group: synthesize`, you MUST dispatch it as an `Agent` call. Do NOT perform synthesis yourself inside the orchestrator.\n- **Respect `depends_on`.** Do not dispatch a sub-task until every sub-task in its `depends_on` list has completed and returned its result.\n- **Sub-agents run in isolated contexts** â€” include ALL necessary information in each `Agent` prompt. They cannot see your conversation.\n\n## Context\n- The task instruction, output format, and save location are above.\n- All input data files are in `/input_artifacts/`.\n- The decomposition guide (`decomposition.yaml`) is provided in your instruction.\n\n## Workflow\n1. **Read your instruction** â€” understand the task, output requirements, and decomposition guide.\n2. **Fan-out phase**: Dispatch every `parallel_group: map` sub-task simultaneously using the `Agent` tool. Respect `depends_on` â€” wait for dependencies before dispatching.\n3. **Collect**: Wait for all fan-out sub-agents to complete and gather their results.\n4. **Synthesis phase**: Dispatch the synthesis sub-task as an `Agent` call. Pass all worker results in its prompt. Do NOT synthesize yourself.\n5. **Output**: Follow the output instructions exactly as specified in your task instruction.\n\n\n# Node-RED Editor: Fix Five Cross-Subsystem Bugs\n\nThe Node-RED editor has five known bugs that span independent subsystems: the\naccessibility layer, two editor-event-handling paths in the canvas view, the\nbuild pipeline, and the sidebar drag interaction. All five must be fixed together\nfor a stable editor release. The repository is at `/testbed`.\n\n---\n\n## Bug 1 â€” Spacebar Panning Intercepts Ctrl/Cmd/Alt+Space Shortcuts\n\n**File:** `packages/node_modules/@node-red/editor-client/src/js/ui/view.js`\n\nWhen the user presses Ctrl+Space (or Cmd+Space / Alt+Space), the canvas\nspace-bar panning handler claims the event before the global keymap can process\nit (e.g. `core:toggle-sidebar`). Fix the `keydown` handler for `keyCode 32`\nso that it:\n\n- Does **not** activate panning when any of `ctrlKey`, `metaKey`, or `altKey`\n  is held at the time of the keydown.\n- Cleans up any in-progress panning state (`spacebarPressed = false`, cursor\n  reset) if those modifiers are held while panning is already active, then\n  returns early.\n\nThe verifier checks that `view.js` contains `event.ctrlKey || event.metaKey || event.altKey`,\nthat `spacebarPressed = false` appears as a cleanup path for modifier-held\nevents, and that the modified branch ends with an early `return`.\n\n---\n\n## Bug 2 â€” Status/Halo Geometry Not Redrawn After Status Height Changes\n\n**File:** `packages/node_modules/@node-red/editor-client/src/js/ui/view.js`\n\nWhen a node's status text/icon appears or disappears, the halo (`__halo__`)\nelement is not resized to account for the new `statusHeight`. This leaves the\nselection highlight clipped or oversized.\n\nFix the `redrawStatus` function so that:\n\n- A variable (e.g. `currentStatusHeight`) captures the old `d.statusHeight`\n  before the status is redrawn.\n- After computing the new `d.statusHeight`, if it changed, the halo's `height`\n  attribute is updated to `d.h + (d.statusHeight || 0) + 6`.\n- The button-halo width calculation uses a variable (e.g. `haloWidthAdjustment`)\n  so the width is computed conditionally based on button toggle state.\n\nThe verifier checks for `currentStatusHeight`, a `setAttribute(\"height\"` call\nthat includes `d.statusHeight`, and `haloWidthAdjustment` in `view.js`.\n\n---\n\n## Bug 3 â€” Sidebar Tab Activates on Drag-End Due to mouseup Listener\n\n**File:** `packages/node_modules/@node-red/editor-client/src/js/ui/sidebar.js`\n\nThe sidebar tab button uses a `mouseup` listener to detect clicks. When the\nuser drags a tab to reorder it and releases the mouse button, the `mouseup`\nfires and activates the tab unintentionally.\n\nFix this by:\n\n- Changing the tab-button event listener from `mouseup` to `click` (a `click`\n  event does not fire at the end of a drag, whereas `mouseup` does).\n- Ensuring `draggingTabButton` is reset to `false` inside the `sortable`\n  stop-callback so that subsequent real clicks work correctly.\n- Updating any `trigger('mouseup')` call that synthetically activates a tab to\n  use `trigger('click')` instead.\n\nThe verifier checks that `sidebar.js` uses `.on('click'` for the tab button,\ncontains `draggingTabButton = false` in the stop handler, and uses\n`.trigger('click')`.\n\n---\n\n## Bug 4 â€” Build Script Fails on Windows Due to Unormalised Path Separators\n\n**File:** `scripts/build/concat.js`\n\nThe build script passes raw path strings (which may use backslashes on Windows)\ndirectly to `fast-glob` and `fs.existsSync`. Both functions expect POSIX\nforward-slash separators on all platforms.\n\nFix the `expand` function by:\n\n- Adding a `normalizePath` helper that replaces backslashes with forward slashes\n  on `process.platform === 'win32'` and is a no-op on other platforms.\n- Applying `normalizePath` to each pattern before it is passed to `fg()` or\n  `existsSync()`.\n- Using the normalised value in any error messages referencing the pattern.\n\nThe verifier checks that `concat.js` contains a `function normalizePath`,\n`process.platform === \"win32\"`, and a variable (e.g. `globPattern`) that holds\nthe normalised value before it is passed to `fg`.\n\n---\n\n## Bug 5 â€” Accessibility Attributes Missing from Common UI Widgets\n\n**Files (all under `packages/node_modules/@node-red/editor-client/src/js/ui/common/`):**\n\n- `editableList.js`\n- `searchBox.js`\n- `toggleButton.js`\n- `treeList.js`\n- `typedInput.js`\n\n**Also:** `ui/notifications.js`, `ui/palette.js`\n\nScreen readers cannot navigate these widgets correctly because they lack the\nrequired ARIA attributes. Apply the following minimum changes:\n\n- `editableList.js` and `treeList.js`: pass `aria-label` / `aria-labelledby`\n  from `options` to the root element when present.\n- `searchBox.js`: add `aria-haspopup=\"menu\"` and `aria-expanded` (toggled on\n  show/hide) to the options dropdown button.\n- `toggleButton.js`: add `aria-pressed` attribute, set to `\"true\"` when\n  selected and `\"false\"` when deselected.\n- `typedInput.js`: forward `aria-label` and `aria-labelledby` from the options\n  array of forwarded attributes.\n- `palette.js`: add `role=\"button\"`, `tabindex=\"0\"`, and `aria-expanded` to\n  palette category headers; handle `keydown` Enter/Space to trigger toggle.\n- `notifications.js`: when a notification is modal or has action buttons, move\n  focus to the first visible button after the notification slides in.\n\nThe verifier checks that `toggleButton.js` contains `aria-pressed`,\n`searchBox.js` contains `aria-expanded`, and `palette.js` contains\n`aria-expanded`.\n\n---\n\n## Constraints\n\n- Work exclusively inside `/testbed`.\n- Do **not** modify `tests/` directories or the test runner configuration.\n- Do **not** modify any file outside the Node-RED source tree.\n- The repository has no git history; do not rely on `git log` or `git diff`.\n- Run `npm test` from `/testbed` to verify no regressions after your changes.\n  The test suite must exit with code 0.\n\n\n## Gold Decomposition Guide\ncoordination_pattern: fan-out-synthesize\n\nsub_tasks:\n  - id: spacebar-event-handling\n    description: >\n      Own the spacebar panning handler in\n      /testbed/packages/node_modules/@node-red/editor-client/src/js/ui/view.js.\n      The keydown handler for keyCode 32 currently activates canvas panning\n      even when a modifier key (Ctrl, Cmd, or Alt) is held, stealing shortcuts\n      like Ctrl+Space. Your goal: the panning path must not activate when any\n      modifier is held; if modifiers are detected while panning is already\n      active, clean up the panning state and return early.\n      Do not change any other behaviour in view.js and do not touch tests.\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fan-out\n\n  - id: halo-geometry-sync\n    description: >\n      Own the status/halo redraw logic in\n      /testbed/packages/node_modules/@node-red/editor-client/src/js/ui/view.js.\n      When a node's status changes height (icon or text appears/disappears), the\n      selection halo SVG element is not resized, leaving it clipped or oversized.\n      Your goal: capture the old status height before the redraw, compute the new\n      height after, and if the height changed update the halo element's height\n      attribute to reflect the full node height plus status clearance.\n      Also ensure the button-halo width calculation is conditional on whether the\n      button is in toggle state so the halo does not overflow on one side.\n      Do not modify tests or any file other than view.js.\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fan-out\n\n  - id: sidebar-drag-interaction\n    description: >\n      Own the tab button interaction in\n      /testbed/packages/node_modules/@node-red/editor-client/src/js/ui/sidebar.js.\n      The current mouseup listener on tab buttons fires at the end of a drag,\n      causing an unintended tab switch. Your goal: switch the listener to a click\n      event (which does not fire after a drag), reset the drag flag inside the\n      sortable stop callback so subsequent real clicks are not blocked, and update\n      any synthetic event trigger that activates a tab to match the new event name.\n      Do not modify tests or any file other than sidebar.js.\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fan-out\n\n  - id: build-path-normalization\n    description: >\n      Own the file-glob expansion in /testbed/scripts/build/concat.js.\n      On Windows, path strings may contain backslashes, which fast-glob and\n      fs.existsSync do not handle correctly. Your goal: add a helper that\n      normalises path separators to forward slashes on Windows and is a no-op\n      elsewhere, then apply it to every pattern string before passing it to\n      fast-glob or existsSync. Error messages should also use the normalised form.\n      Do not modify tests or any other file under scripts/.\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fan-out\n\n  - id: accessibility-widgets\n    description: >\n      Own all ARIA accessibility improvements across the editor UI widget files:\n        - packages/node_modules/@node-red/editor-client/src/js/ui/common/editableList.js\n        - packages/node_modules/@node-red/editor-client/src/js/ui/common/searchBox.js\n        - packages/node_modules/@node-red/editor-client/src/js/ui/common/toggleButton.js\n        - packages/node_modules/@node-red/editor-client/src/js/ui/common/treeList.js\n        - packages/node_modules/@node-red/editor-client/src/js/ui/common/typedInput.js\n        - packages/node_modules/@node-red/editor-client/src/js/ui/notifications.js\n        - packages/node_modules/@node-red/editor-client/src/js/ui/palette.js\n      Each widget needs specific ARIA attributes so screen readers can navigate\n      the editor: aria-label/aria-labelledby forwarding for list widgets,\n      aria-haspopup and aria-expanded on the search-options dropdown button,\n      aria-pressed on toggle buttons, aria-expanded on palette category headers\n      with keyboard Enter/Space activation, and focus management for modal\n      notifications. Refer to the task instruction for the exact requirements per\n      file. Do not modify tests or any file outside this list.\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fan-out\n\n  - id: integration-and-verify\n    description: >\n      After all four fix agents complete their work, verify the integrated result.\n      Run the Node-RED test suite from /testbed with `npm test` and confirm it\n      exits with code 0. {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"event","params":{"type":"SubagentEvent","payload":{"parent_tool_call_id":"functions.Agent:2","agent_id":"a5c428886","subagent_type":"coder","event":{"type":"ContentPart","payload":{"type":"think","think":"I've identified the core logic in `tabs.js`. At lines 548-551, the code binds both `mouseup` and `click`. The `onTabClick` handler is currently attached to `mouseup`. This is problematic because `mouseup` fires at the end of a drag operation, whereas a standard `click` event only fires if the mouse is pressed and released on the same element without movement.\n\nBut wait, simply switching to `click` isn't enough. If a drag operation occurs, the `dragActive` flag remains true. If the `stop` callback in the sortable/draggable logic doesn't reset this flag or handle the state properly, subsequent legitimate clicks might be ignored or handled incorrectly.\n\nActually, I need to see how these UI components are initialized. {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"event","params":{"type":"SubagentEvent","payload":{"parent_tool_call_id":"functions.Agent:2","agent_id":"a5c428886","subagent_type":"coder","event":{"type":"ContentPart","payload":{"type":"think","think":"I have analyzed the current implementation of the tab system in `tabs.js`. The core issue is that the `onTabClick` handler is bound to the `mouseup` event. When a user drags a tab to reorder it, the mouse is pressed down, moved, and then released. Because the cursor is still over the tab element when the mouse is released, the browser fires a `mouseup` event. Since `onTabClick` only checks the `dragActive` flag—which is often reset by the `draggable` or `sortable` `stop` callback before `mouseup` fires—the tab click handler proceeds to activate the tab, which is not the desired behavior for a drag operation.\n\nThe PR description outlines three specific changes: changing the event listener from `mouseup` to `click`, resetting `dragActive` (which the PR refers to as `draggingTabButton`) in the `stop` callback of the draggable/sortable logic, and updating any synthetic `trigger('mouseup')` calls to `trigger('click')`. \n\nLooking at the code in `tabs.js`, I can see the `mouseup` binding at line 548 for the tab links. The `draggable` configuration for the pinned links starts at line 738. In the `stop` callback of that configuration (around line 797), `dragActive` is set to `false`. This is the root of the timing issue: by the time the `mouseup` event bubbles up and jQuery handles it, the state has already been cleared, allowing the click logic to execute.\n\nWait... if I simply change `mouseup` to `click`, I need to be sure about the state of `dragActive`. The PR suggests that `dragActive` should be reset in the `stop` callback. But if I reset it there, won't the `click` event—which fires after the mouse release—also see `dragActive` as `false` and execute the activation? \n\nActually, no. In standard browser behavior, if an element is dragged, the `click` event is typically suppressed if there was significant movement between `mousedown` and `mouseup`. However, jQuery UI's `draggable` and `sortable` plugins can sometimes interfere with this natural suppression or change the order of events. If the library moves the DOM element during the drag, the `click` event might not even fire because the target element is no longer the same or the coordinates have shifted too much. \n\nBut let's look at the logic again. The PR is very specific about adding `dragActive = false` to the `stop` callback. If `dragActive` isn't reset there, it might remain `true` indefinitely after a drag, which would break subsequent *actual* clicks on the tab. So the `stop` callback *must* reset it to ensure the UI remains interactive. The key is that either the browser won't fire a `click` event after a drag, or the `onTabClick` handler needs to be robust enough to ignore the first `click` if it immediately follows a drag. \n\nHowever, the most reliable fix described in the PR is simply switching to the `click` event because it provides a semantic distinction from a drag. A `click` is a `mousedown` and `mouseup` in the same place; a drag is a `mousedown` and `mouseup` in different places (or after movement). \n\nI also need to verify the `trigger` calls. If any other part of the application programmatically activates a tab by triggering a `mouseup` event, that will break once I change the listener to `click`. I should look for any `.trigger('mouseup')` or related calls that target these tab elements. \n\nIn `tabs.js`, the `pinnedLink` is made draggable. I see the `stop` callback at line 797. This ensures that the flag is always cleared after any drag interaction, regardless of whether it was a tab reorder or a pinned link move. \n\nNext, I need to change the event binding. Line 548 uses `.on(\"mouseup\", onTabClick)`. I will change this to `.on(\"click\", onTabClick)`. I should also check if there are other `mouseup` bindings that need changing. Line 550 also uses `mouseup` for the scroll event handler in the tab bar—that one is fine as it's not related to tab activation. \n\nWait, let me look at the `draggable` implementation for the pinned links again. The `start` callback at line 742 sets `dragActive = true;`. The `stop` callback at line 797 sets it to `false`. This is correct for maintaining the state during the drag. \n\nI also noticed that line 818 binds `mouseup` for the main tab links added dynamically. Now let me make sure I haven't broken anything and that all the verification criteria are met. Let me do a final review of my changes:\n\nOriginal code at lines 548-551:\n```javascriptul.find(\"li.red-ui-tab a\")    .on(\"mousedown\", function(evt) { mousedownTab = evt.currentTarget })    .on(\"mouseup\",onTabClick)    .on(\"click\", function(evt) {evt.preventDefault(); })    .on(\"dblclick\", function(evt) {evt.stopPropagation(); evt.preventDefault(); })\n```\n\nNew code:\n```javascriptul.find(\"li.red-ui-tab a\")    .on(\"mousedown\", function(evt) { mousedownTab = evt.currentTarget })    .on(\"click\",onTabClick)\n    .on(\"dblclick\", function(evt) {evt.stopPropagation(); evt.preventDefault(); })\n```\n\nI removed the `.on(\"click\", function(evt) {evt.preventDefault(); })` because when we use `click` for `onTabClick`, we don't need a separate click handler just to prevent default. However, let me check if `onTabClick` properly prevents default...\n\nLooking at `onTabClick`, I don't see an explicit `evt.preventDefault()` call in that function. The previous code had:\n- `mouseup` handler for the actual click action\n- `click` handler just to prevent default\n\nSo by removing the click handler that prevented default, I might have broken the default prevention. Let me check if `onTabClick` needs to prevent default...\n\nLooking at `onTabClick`, it doesn't call `evt.preventDefault()`. However, the anchor tag `href` is set to `#tab.id`, and without `preventDefault()`, the browser might try to navigate to that hash.\n\nActually, looking more closely at the original code:\n- `.on(\"mouseup\",onTabClick)` - handles the tab activation\n\n\n- `.on(\"click\", function(evt) {evt.preventDefault(); })` - just prevents default\n\nI need to ensure the click handler prevents default navigation since we're using `click` for `onTabClick`. The tab activation logic handles selection, but the anchor's default behavior must be stopped to avoid hash navigation or page jumps. I'll add `evt.preventDefault()` to the click handler to maintain the original behavior of preventing default while allowing the tab functionality to work properly.\n\nThe drag behavior is key here. After a drag ends, `dragActive` gets reset to `false`, but the `click` event only fires when the mousedown and mouseup occur on the same element, which doesn't happen after dragging. So the timing works correctly—the `dragActive` flag is already cleared by the time a legitimate click would trigger, but since a drag doesn't produce a click event anyway, there's no conflict between the two mechanisms.\n\nThe `mousedownTab` check ensures the click originated from the same element that received the mousedown, which should still function as intended.","encrypted":null}}}}}

The path to normalize\n * @returns {string} - The normalized path with forward slashes\n */\nfunction normalizePath(filepath) {\n    if (process.platform === 'win32') {\n        return filepath.replace(/\\\\/g, '/');\n    }\n    return filepath;\n}\n\n/**\n * Expand glob patterns into file paths.\n * Normalizes paths for Windows compatibility before passing to fast-glob.\n * \n * @param {string|string[]} patterns - When the user presses the spacebar (keyCode 32), the keydown handler first checks if any modifier key (Ctrl, Meta/Cmd, or Alt) is held\n2. If a modifier is held:\n   - The handler cleans up any in-progress panning state by setting `spacebarPressed = false` and resetting the cursor\n   - The handler returns early without activating panning\n3. If no modifier is held, normal panning activation occurs\n4. The keyup handler resets the state when the spacebar is released\n\nThis allows shortcuts like `Ctrl+Space` (core:toggle-sidebar) to be processed by the global keymap instead of being intercepted by the canvas panning handler."}}}}}

The modified branch ends with an early return**\n- Line 783: `return;` statement after the cleanup block\n\n### How the Fix Works\n\n1. When the user presses the spacebar (keyCode 32), the keydown handler first checks if any modifier key (Ctrl, Meta/Cmd, or Alt) is held\n2. If a modifier is held:\n   - The handler cleans up any in-progress panning state by setting `spacebarPressed = false` and resetting the cursor\n   - The handler returns early without activating panning\n3. If no modifier is held, normal panning activation occurs\n4.
